prompt: Create a realistic side-by-side comparison image showing the same cafe portrait duplicated in two equal vertical panels, like a before-and-after social media demonstration for removing glasses glare while preserving identity. Canvas is a wide 16:9 image, approximately 1200×675, split exactly down the center by a thin vertical white divider. Both panels show one adult man seated at a cafe table by large windows, wearing a dark navy blazer over a plain white crew-neck T-shirt, arms resting forward, with short neatly styled black hair and warm medium skin tone. In both panels, place a large opaque tan square censorship block over the center of the face, covering the eyes, nose, and mouth while leaving hair, ears, jawline, neck, and clothing visible; use muted tan brown. The left panel should subtly imply the original portrait with eyeglass frames and glare partly visible at the edges of the face block, while the right panel should show the cleaned version with no visible glasses glare and the same face shape, pose, hair, outfit, lighting, and background. Include exactly 2 portrait panels: left original and right edited. Include exactly 2 identical coffee cups, one near the bottom center of each panel, cream-colored ceramic with coffee inside. Include exactly 2 small green potted plants, one at the lower left of each panel. Background: bright cafe interior with black window frames, sunlit glass, soft green foliage outside, blurred street and building shapes, shallow depth of field, natural daylight, realistic photography. Style should be high-quality candid profile-photo realism, crisp subject details, soft bokeh background, no text labels, no watermark, no extra people, and no changes between the two panels except the suggested glasses-glare removal on the right. Use dark navy blazer over a white T-shirt, sunlit cafe by large windows, and glasses glare removal before and after.
